title: Midnight at the Airport
content: Airports often feel chaotic during the day, but after midnight they become entirely different spaces. Ethan discovered this while waiting for a delayed flight in a nearly empty terminal. Most shops had closed, and only a handful of travelers remained scattered among rows of seats. Cleaning crews moved quietly through the building while departure screens glowed in the darkness. Outside, airplanes rested motionless beneath bright lights. Ethan watched the scene unfold and felt suspended between places, neither fully departed nor fully arrived. There was something strangely peaceful about those hours. The airport no longer felt like a transportation hub. Instead, it resembled a temporary world inhabited by people caught between destinations and possibilities.
